name: Microsoft Clarity
what_it_does: Free Microsoft tool for website behavior analytics providing heatmaps, session recordings, and click/scroll analytics to understand user interaction patterns.
use_cases: Analyzing user behavior data via Clarity API to feed insights into content optimization agents
Exporting session recording metadata for AI-powered UX analysis pipelines
Monitoring rage click and dead click patterns as quality signals for UI agents
Integrating Clarity heatmap data with A/B testing agents for conversion optimization
Querying aggregated page analytics for agent-driven website performance reporting
not_for: Programmatic access to raw session data at scale (Clarity API is limited vs Hotjar/FullStory)
Real-time event streaming (data is processed asynchronously with delay)
HIPAA-regulated healthcare sites (session recordings capture all user input)
best_when: You need completely free heatmaps and session recordings for a web application with basic API access for aggregated metrics.
avoid_when: You need advanced segmentation, cohort analysis, or programmatic access to rich session data — use FullStory or Hotjar.

auth.notes: Azure AD OAuth2 for API access. JavaScript snippet for web tracking (no auth on client-side tracking).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — unlimited sessions, heatmaps, and recordings
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier — Clarity is free
pricing.notes: Microsoft offers Clarity free to drive Azure/Microsoft ecosystem adoption. No paid tier or usage limits on session tracking.

performance.rate_limits: API access rate limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited API documentation; error responses are standard Azure AD format
known_agent_gotchas: Clarity API is very limited compared to UI — most data only accessible through dashboard, not programmatically
Data is available via API with 24-48 hour delay — not suitable for real-time analytics agents
Session recordings are not accessible via API — only aggregated heatmap and metrics data
Azure AD OAuth required even for simple read access — setup overhead disproportionate to API capability
GDPR consent banner must be in place before deploying Clarity — tracks all user interactions including form inputs

security_score_components.security_notes: Session recordings capture all user input by default — implement masking for sensitive fields. Data stored by Microsoft.
